title: New Testament Foundations — Matthew
subtitle: The Gospel of the Promised King
description: A structured study of Matthew's Gospel tracing Jesus as the promised Messiah who fulfills Israel's Scripture, establishes the kingdom of heaven, and commissions his disciples to all nations.
learning_outcomes: Identify Matthew's major fulfillment citations and their OT sources
Explain the structure and theology of the Sermon on the Mount
Describe the five major discourse blocks in Matthew
Trace the kingdom-of-heaven theme across the Gospel
Explain the significance of Jesus' death and resurrection in Matthew's narrative

theological_framework.emphasis: Jesus as fulfillment of Israel's covenant history

units.title: The Kingdom Proclaimed
units.description: Sermon on the Mount and early ministry (Lessons 4–7)

instructional_approach.session_structure: 7-segment lesson flow (orientation, reading, context, analysis, themes, question, close)
instructional_approach.knowledge_assessment: factual recall questions at lesson end

questions: What two titles open Matthew's genealogy in 1:1?
Which OT prophet does Matthew cite for the virgin birth?
What is the first beatitude in the Sermon on the Mount?
What is the primary theme of Matthew's parables in chapter 13?
What commission does Jesus give his disciples in Matthew 28:19-20?

next_course.title: New Testament Foundations — Mark
next_course.description: Study the Gospel of Mark's portrait of Jesus as the urgent, authoritative Servant
